note.soap:
S:  
Patient called with an unspecified chief complaint. No additional symptom details or history were provided. No known medication allergies reported.

O:  
No physical exam findings available due to phone-only encounter. No vital signs or objective data provided.

A:  
- Working diagnosis (most likely): Unable to determine due to lack of symptom information.  
- Differential: Cannot be established without further clinical details.  
- Red flags considered: None identified due to absence of symptom data.

P:  
- Disposition: Recommend patient provide more detailed symptom history or seek in-person evaluation if symptoms worsen or new concerning features develop.  
- Plan: Await further information to clarify clinical picture before making diagnostic or treatment decisions.  
- Safety net: Advise patient to call or present urgently if experiencing severe pain, difficulty breathing, chest pain, sudden weakness, altered mental status, high fever, or other emergent symptoms.
patient_instructions: If you are not improving as expected, or if symptoms worsen, seek in-person medical care.
Medication options you may consider if safe (no dosing by phone): acetaminophen (Tylenol) if you can take it safely; ibuprofen (Advil) if you can take it safely.
